title: carex rostrata f. elatior
intro: Carex rostrata f. elatior, also known as the tall beaked sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Carex in the family Cyperaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, which can help to treat a range of health issues, including urinary tract infections and skin conditions. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting urinary system health, and it is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions and tinctures. In herbal medicine, Carex rostrata f. elatior is typically harvested in the summer months, dried, and then processed into a range of products, including teas, capsules, and topical creams.
